Re:
Farn PolrrrcAt- PRACrrcEs C ovrrrarssroN
P.O. Box 807 t 428 J Street o Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
November 14,2001
John Sulpizio, Port Director
Port of Sacramento
Post Office Box 980070
West Sacramento, CA 9 57 98-007 0
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-01-212
Dear Mr. Sulpizio
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").r This letter is limited solely to provisions of the Act,
and should not be taken as advice or an opinion regarding any other areas ofthe law
potentially rai.qed by your letter. T'his advice is based upon the facts as provided in your
request letter.2 Please note that this letter should not be construed to evaluate any
conduct that has already taken place. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A) )
QUESTION
For purposes of the financial disclosure requirements under $ 87302, what is the
jurisdiction of the Port of Sacramento3'?
CONCLUSION
The jurisdiction of the Port District is all of the land within its geographical
boundaries, as well as any real property that has been acquired by eminent domain or
other means. For purposes of financial disclosure, real property or any part of it that is
1 Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
I 8 1 09- 1 8 997, of the California Code of Regulations.
2 This advice is applicable and confers 
only to the extent that the facts provided to us are
correct, and that all of the material facts have been disclosed. (In re Oglesby (I975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71;
$ 83114.)
3 The stattrtory denomination for the Port of Sacramento is the Sacramento-Yolo Port District
(herein referred to as the "Port District").
Lq4


File No. A-01-212
Page No. 2
within, or not more than two miles outside the boundaries of the Port District, or other
real property acquired or used by it, is deemed to be within its jurisdiction.
FACTS
You are the port director for the Port of Sacramento. As the port director, you file
annual statements of economic interest ("SEI") pursuant to Government Code $ 87302.
The geographic boundaries of the port district are all of the lands within Sacramento
County and the ls Supervisorial District of Yolo County. The port district has also
annexed or acquired by eminent domain some other properties.
To your knowledge, the port has no power or jurisdiction in aly area outside the
port's immediate geographic area, except over the above mentioned properties that
previously were annexed or acquired through the exercise of eminent domain or by some
other means. Further, the port has no current plans to exercise eminent domain power.
ANALYSIS
Under 5 87302, a local public agerrcy, such as the Port District, must have a
conflict of interest code that requires "designated employees" to disclose "interests in real
property." (See also S 87206.) The definition of an "interest in real property" states that
it must be "located in the jurisdiction." ($ 82033.) Jurisdiction is defined as follows:
"'Jurisdiction' means the state with respect to a state agency and, with respect to a
local government agency, the region, county, city, district or other geographical
area in which it has jurisdiction. Real property shall be deemed to be 'within
the jurisdiction' with respect to a local government agency if the property or any
part of it is located within or not more than two miles outside the boundaries of
the jurisdiction or within two miles of any land owned or used by the local
government agency." ($ 82035, emphasis added.)
Applying these provisions to the Port District, its jurisdiction, for purposes of
disclosure of real property interests by designated employees, would be within or not
more than two miles outside of the Port District's geographic boundaries, or other real
property acquired or used by it. (See Biondo Advice Letter, No. A-86-238 )
You have expressed a concern that the Port District's extra-territorial powers of
eminent domain may enlarge the scope of its jurisdiction for financial disclosure
purposes. As a local public enttty, the Port District "may acquire property by eminent
domain outside its territorial limits for wateE gas, or electric supply purposes or for
airports, drainage or sewer purposes if it is authorized to acquire property by eminent
domain for the purposes for which the property is to be acquired." (Code of Civ. Pro.
$ 1240.125 ) However, until such power is exercised, and the property is acquired, it
would not come within the definition of "jurisdiction" as set forth in $ 82035. (See
Takahoshi Advice Letter,I-90-535.) The possibility that sometime in the future the Port
District may expand its jurisdiction by the use of eminent domain or other forms of


File No. A-01-212
Page No. 3
property acquisition does not alter its presentjurisdictional boundaries. Therefore, no
increased frnancial disclosure obligation for designated employees would flow from the
Port District's unexercised power of eminent domain.a
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
L.
Jr
Staff Counsel, Legal Division
WLW:jg
I:\Adviceltrs\01-212
a Irrespective of whether a given financial interest is required to be reported on a statemenl of
econontic iuterests, a public offlrcial still may not make or participate in making a governrnental decision
that will have a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on his or her economic interest. ($$ 87100,
87103.)
